rationale: Candidate's 20+ years of data engineering experience, expertise in Python, SQL, and AWS, and recent roles in ETL pipeline design align strongly with the job's focus on Spark-based data processing and pipeline optimization on Azure Databricks. Minor gaps in Scala and Azure-specific experience are offset by transferable skills.

responsibilities: Design, develop, and maintain large-scale Spark applications
Build and optimize batch and streaming data pipelines
Write production-quality Spark code focusing on performance and scalability
Optimize Spark jobs (partitioning, caching, memory tuning)
Collaborate with platform, analytics, and data science teams
Debug and troubleshoot Spark job issues in production
Follow best practices for code quality, testing, and documentation
requirements_text: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent experience
3+ years of experience as a Data Engineer or similar role
Strong hands-on experience with Apache Spark
Proficiency in Python, Scala, or Java
Experience with data lakes and columnar formats
Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail
risk_flags: Limited direct experience with Scala and Azure-specific technologies
On-site requirement in Dallas may conflict with candidate's remote preference

tailoring_plan.positioning_angle: Highlight 20+ years of data engineering expertise, focusing on Python, SQL, and ETL pipeline optimization as directly transferable to Spark-based data processing. Emphasize adaptability to learn Scala and Azure Databricks based on strong foundation in distributed systems and AWS.
priority_experiences: Architected automated ETL pipelines (Python/Pandas) at CITI, ingesting telemetry from 6,000+ endpoints
Designed optimized Oracle schemas for historical retention and forecasting at CITI
Managed performance engineering and data mining for optimization at G6 Hospitality
bullet_strategy: Lead with Python and SQL expertise in context of large-scale data pipeline design
Frame AWS and PySpark experience as transferable to Azure Databricks and Spark environments
Address Scala gap by emphasizing quick learning of new languages (e.g., Java, C++) and adaptability
Mitigate on-site concern by expressing flexibility for hybrid or relocation to Dallas if required
